# Configuration reference

<!-- Generated by tools/gen_config_docs.py — do not edit by hand. -->

Every setting myMeal understands. This table is generated from the same
inventory the application validates against, so it cannot drift.

## Precedence

Highest wins:

1. **Explicit config** passed to `create_app()` (tests, embedding).
2. **Home Assistant add-on options** (`/data/options.json`).
3. **Environment variables** (`MYMEAL_*`, or `MYMEAL_*_FILE` for secrets).
4. **Built-in defaults.**

Home Assistant options outrank the environment because, inside the add-on,
options.json is the only surface an operator can edit — if the environment
won, toggling a switch in the HA UI would silently do nothing. Outside Home
Assistant the file does not exist, so the environment is authoritative.

An empty environment variable (`MYMEAL_AI_PROVIDER=`) means *unset*, and
falls through to the default rather than failing validation.

## Validation

Configuration is validated once at startup, before the database is opened
and before any port is bound. Invalid configuration prints **every** problem
and refuses to start — it never surfaces later as a request-time 500.

Booleans accept `1 true yes on` / `0 false no off` (any case). Anything else
is an error: an unrecognised value is never silently treated as false.

Check a deployment without starting it:

```bash
docker compose run --rm app python3 -m app.config_check
```

## Settings

### Storage

| Variable | Type | Default | Secret | Restart | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MYMEAL_DATA_DIR` | string | `./data` | no | yes | Directory for the SQLite database and uploaded images. |
| `MYMEAL_DATABASE_URL` | string | `_(empty)_` | no | yes | Full SQLAlchemy URL. Blank = SQLite inside DATA_DIR. Only SQLite is tested/supported today. |

### Security

| Variable | Type | Default | Secret | Restart | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MYMEAL_SECRET_KEY` | string | `_(empty)_` | **yes** | yes | JWT signing key. Blank = generated once and persisted in DATA_DIR. Supports `MYMEAL_SECRET_KEY_FILE`. |
| `MYMEAL_JWT_HOURS` | enum/int | `168` | no | yes | How long an issued token stays valid, in hours. |
| `MYMEAL_DISABLE_AUTH` | boolean | `false` | no | yes | Bind every request to a single local user. ONLY safe behind an authenticating proxy such as Home Assistant ingress. HA option: `disable_auth`. |
| `MYMEAL_ALLOW_REGISTRATION` | boolean | `true` | no | yes | Allow anyone who can reach the app to create an account. HA option: `allow_registration`. |

### Network & proxy

| Variable | Type | Default | Secret | Restart | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MYMEAL_PORT` | enum/int | `7850` | no | yes | HTTP port for the app. |
| `MYMEAL_CORS_ORIGINS` | list | `_(empty)_` | no | yes | Comma-separated origins allowed to make credentialed cross-origin requests. Empty = same-origin only (correct for normal deployments). |
| `MYMEAL_TRUSTED_PROXY_COUNT` | enum/int | `0` | no | yes | How many reverse proxies sit in front. 0 = do not trust X-Forwarded-* headers at all. |

### AI providers

| Variable | Type | Default | Secret | Restart | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MYMEAL_AI_PROVIDER` | enum/int | `_(empty)_` | no | yes | Which AI backend to use. Blank disables AI features cleanly. HA option: `ai_provider`. |
| `MYMEAL_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | string | `_(empty)_` | **yes** | yes | API key for the claude provider. HA option: `anthropic_api_key`. Supports `MYMEAL_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_FILE`. |
| `MYMEAL_CLAUDE_MODEL` | string | `claude-opus-4-8` | no | yes | Model for the claude provider. HA option: `claude_model`. |
| `MYMEAL_OPENAI_API_KEY` | string | `_(empty)_` | **yes** | yes | API key for the openai provider. HA option: `openai_api_key`. Supports `MYMEAL_OPENAI_API_KEY_FILE`. |
| `MYMEAL_OPENAI_MODEL` | string | `gpt-4o-mini` | no | yes | Model for the openai provider. HA option: `openai_model`. |
| `MYMEAL_OPENAI_BASE_URL` | string | `_(empty)_` | no | yes | Override the OpenAI API base URL (for compatible gateways). |
| `MYMEAL_OLLAMA_HOST` | url | `http://localhost:11434` | no | yes | Base URL of the Ollama server. Local, so no API key is needed. HA option: `ollama_host`. |
| `MYMEAL_OLLAMA_MODEL` | string | `llama3.1` | no | yes | Model for the ollama provider. HA option: `ollama_model`. |
| `MYMEAL_AI_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | enum/int | `60` | no | yes | Per-request timeout for AI provider calls. |

### MCP (Home Assistant Assist)

| Variable | Type | Default | Secret | Restart | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MYMEAL_MCP_ENABLED` | boolean | `true` | no | yes | Run the MCP server so Home Assistant Assist can use myMeal as a tool. HA option: `enable_mcp`. |
| `MYMEAL_MCP_HOST` | string | `0.0.0.0` | no | yes | Bind address for the MCP server. |
| `MYMEAL_MCP_PORT` | enum/int | `7851` | no | yes | Port for the MCP server (SSE). |
| `MYMEAL_MCP_API` | string | `_(empty)_` | no | yes | Backend API URL the MCP server calls. Blank = derived from PORT. |
| `MYMEAL_MCP_API_TOKEN` | string | `_(empty)_` | **yes** | yes | Token the MCP server uses when app auth is enabled. Supports `MYMEAL_MCP_API_TOKEN_FILE`. |
| `MYMEAL_MCP_REQUIRED` | boolean | `false` | no | yes | Treat a dead MCP process as making the whole app UNREADY. |

### Edibl integration (companion inventory app)

| Variable | Type | Default | Secret | Restart | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MYMEAL_EDIBL_URL` | string | `_(empty)_` | no | yes | Base URL of a companion Edibl instance (e.g. http://edibl:8080). Blank disables the integration. When set, myMeal can pull real stock and push meal-plan ingredients. |
| `MYMEAL_EDIBL_API_TOKEN` | string | `_(empty)_` | **yes** | yes | API token myMeal presents to Edibl (Edibl tokens API). Sent as a Bearer token. Supports `MYMEAL_EDIBL_API_TOKEN_FILE`. |

### Serving

| Variable | Type | Default | Secret | Restart | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MYMEAL_WORKERS` | enum/int | `2` | no | yes | Gunicorn worker processes. SQLite is single-writer: more workers raises write contention, it does not raise write throughput. |
| `MYMEAL_THREADS` | enum/int | `4` | no | yes | Gunicorn threads per worker. |
| `MYMEAL_TIMEOUT` | enum/int | `120` | no | yes | Gunicorn worker timeout (seconds). |
| `MYMEAL_GRACEFUL_TIMEOUT` | enum/int | `30` | no | yes | Seconds to finish in-flight requests on shutdown. |
| `MYMEAL_MAX_UPLOAD_MB` | enum/int | `50` | no | yes | Maximum upload size in MB. |
| `MYMEAL_HTTP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | enum/int | `20` | no | yes | Timeout for outbound HTTP (recipe import). |
| `MYMEAL_MAX_IMPORT_BYTES` | enum/int | `5242880` | no | yes | Maximum bytes read from a page during recipe import. |

### Misc

| Variable | Type | Default | Secret | Restart | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MYMEAL_FRONTEND_DIST` | string | `_(empty)_` | no | yes | Path to the built SPA. Blank = the location baked into the image. |
| `MYMEAL_LOG_LEVEL` | enum/int | `INFO` | no | yes | Application log level. |
| `MYMEAL_DEBUG` | boolean | `false` | no | yes | Flask debug mode. NEVER enable in production — it exposes an interactive debugger that executes arbitrary code. |

## Security notes

- **`MYMEAL_SECRET_KEY`** must be at least 32 characters when
  authentication is enabled, and known placeholders are rejected outright.
  Leave it unset and myMeal generates one **once** and persists it to
  `$MYMEAL_DATA_DIR/.secret_key` (mode 0600). Back that file up with your
  data: losing it logs everyone out and voids every issued API token.
- **`MYMEAL_DISABLE_AUTH=true` binds every request to one local user.** It is
  correct behind Home Assistant ingress and wrong anywhere else. Combining it
  with `MYMEAL_CORS_ORIGINS` is refused outright, because that would let any
  listed website drive the API as that user.
- **CORS is same-origin by default.** The SPA is served by the app itself, so
  no cross-origin access is needed. Only add origins for a separate frontend
  (e.g. the Vite dev server at `http://localhost:5173`). `*` is refused.
- **`X-Forwarded-*` headers are ignored** unless `MYMEAL_TRUSTED_PROXY_COUNT`
  is set, since anyone can send them.
- **Secrets never reach the frontend.** Provider keys are used server-side
  only, and are redacted from logs, `config_check`, and error messages.

## Deployment support

| Setting group | Standalone Docker | Home Assistant add-on |
|---|---|---|
| Storage | yes (mount a volume) | fixed to `/data`, backed up by HA |
| Security | yes | `disable_auth` / `allow_registration` via add-on options |
| AI providers | yes | `ai_provider` via add-on options; keys in the app UI |
| MCP | yes | `enable_mcp` via add-on options |
| Serving | yes | yes (environment) |
